# FastAPI Hexagonal Template

> Production-grade hexagonal-architecture (ports & adapters) FastAPI template where the layer
> boundaries are enforced in CI by import-linter — not merely drawn in a diagram — and built so
> AI coding agents can one-shot features and self-verify against one deterministic command.

Python 3.13, FastAPI, uv, async SQLAlchemy 2.0 + asyncpg, Alembic, Postgres, pydantic v2,
loguru, OpenTelemetry. The `Task` resource is the canonical vertical slice to copy. The
definition of done is `uv run poe check` (ruff → ty → import-linter → deptry → pip-audit →
pytest with coverage and schemathesis), which is deterministic: pinned versions, seeded test
order, derandomized fuzzing.

Key differentiator: unlike full-stack-fastapi-template (not hexagonal, ORM-coupled),
fastapi-best-practices (a guide), and fastapi-clean-example (clean but folder-convention only,
unenforced), this template enforces the inward-only dependency rule as a build gate.

## Docs

- [Home](index.md): overview and quickstart.
- [Architecture](architecture.md): the three layers, the inward-only rule, and CI enforcement.
- [Adding a feature](adding-a-feature.md): domain-outward runbook mirroring the Task slice.
- [Validation loop](validation-loop.md): the one command, each tool, and determinism.
- [Agent-native](agent-native.md): AGENTS.md, .claude assets, the determinism contract.
- [Comparison](comparison.md): versus the popular FastAPI templates, with when-not-to-use.
- [FAQ](faq.md): common questions about ports/adapters, uv, Docker, auth, agents.

## Decision records

- [ADR-0001](adr/0001-enforce-hexagonal-boundaries-with-import-linter.md): enforce boundaries with import-linter.
- [ADR-0002](adr/0002-validation-loop-tool-selection.md): validation-loop tool selection and exclusions.
- [ADR-0003](adr/0003-unit-of-work-commit-authority.md): the UnitOfWork is the sole commit authority.
- [ADR-0004](adr/0004-utc-timestamps-owned-by-the-domain.md): entities own their UTC timestamps.

## Source

- [Repository](https://github.com/MatthiasEg/python-hexagonal-architecture-template)
